Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

who is the "rain god" ?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-11-04 · 1 answers
Growing up in India, what we heard all the time was "Rain God is Varuna" & Indra as God as thunder.
But SB says Indra is rain God http://vedabase.net/sb/10/24/8/ not as "rain god but "controller of rain"

Should that be interpreted as "controller of varuna" ?
user [38] · 2010-11-04
Varuna is in charge of waters in seas - SB 3.18.1, 4.6.10, 10.28.2,10, 10.50.55, 10.65.19, 11.16.17

search string: varuna site:vedabase.net

‹ all questions
